channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Slang Context-based Inference Enhancement via Greedy Search-Guided Chain-of-Thought Prompting
item.description: arXiv:2603.13230v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Slang interpretation has been a challenging downstream task for Large Language Models (LLMs) as the expressions are inherently embedded in contextual, cultural, and linguistic frameworks. In the absence of domain-specific training data, it is difficul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, experiment, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Steering at the Source: Style Modulation Heads for Robust Persona Control
item.description: arXiv:2603.13249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Activation steering offers a computationally efficient mechanism for controlling Large Language Models (LLMs) without fine-tuning. While effectively controlling target traits (e.g., persona), coherency degradation remains a major obstacle to safety an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, large language model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Training-Free Agentic AI: Probabilistic Control and Coordination in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.13256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent large language model (LLM) systems enable complex, long-horizon reasoning by composing specialized agents, but practical deployment remains hindered by inefficient routing, noisy feedback, and high interaction cost. We introduce REDEREF, a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, memory, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Transformers Reject Wrong Answers: Rotational Dynamics of Factual Constraint Processing
item.description: arXiv:2603.13259v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When a language model is fed a wrong answer, what happens inside the network? Current understanding treats truthfulness as a static property of individual-layer representations-a direction to be probed, a feature to be extracted. Less is known about t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, vector, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Explain in Your Own Words: Improving Reasoning via Token-Selective Dual Knowledge Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2603.13260v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge Distillation (KD) can transfer the reasoning abilities of large models to smaller ones, which can reduce the costs to generate Chain-of-Thoughts for reasoning tasks. KD methods typically ask the student to mimic the teacher's distribution ov...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, framework, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Design and evaluation of an agentic workflow for crisis-related synthetic tweet datasets
item.description: arXiv:2603.13625v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Twitter (now X) has become an important source of social media data for situational awareness during crises. Crisis informatics research has widely used tweets from Twitter to develop and evaluate artificial intelligence (AI) systems for various crisi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, research, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Widespread Gender and Pronoun Bias in Moral Judgments Across LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.13636v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to assess moral or ethical statements, yet their judgments may reflect social and linguistic biases. This work presents a controlled, sentence-level study of how grammatical person, number, and gender...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, alignment, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Benchmarking Large Language Models on Reference Extraction and Parsing in the Social Sciences and Humanities
item.description: arXiv:2603.13651v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Bibliographic reference extraction and parsing are foundational for citation indexing, linking, and downstream scholarly knowledge-graph construction. However, most established evaluations focus on clean, English, end-of-document bibliographies, and t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, ICL, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Privacy Preserving Topic-wise Sentiment Analysis of the Iran Israel USA Conflict Using Federated Transformer Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.13655v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The recent escalation of the Iran Israel USA conflict in 2026 has triggered widespread global discussions across social media platforms. As people increasingly use these platforms for expressing opinions, analyzing public sentiment from these discussi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, platform, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Preconditioned Test-Time Adaptation for Out-of-Distribution Debiasing in Narrative Generation
item.description: arXiv:2603.13683v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although debiased LLMs perform well on known bias patterns, they often fail to generalize to unfamiliar bias prompts, producing toxic outputs. We first validate that such high-bias prompts constitute a \emph{distribution shift} via OOD detection, and ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, context, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Human Attribution of Causality to AI Across Agency, Misuse, and Misalignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.13236v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-related incidents are becoming increasingly frequent and severe, ranging from safety failures to misuse by malicious actors. In such complex situations, identifying which elements caused an adverse outcome, the problem of cause selection, is a crit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, alignment, arxiv, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Benchmarking Zero-Shot Reasoning Approaches for Error Detection in Solidity Smart Contracts
item.description: arXiv:2603.13239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Smart contracts play a central role in blockchain systems by encoding financial and operational logic. Still, their susceptibility to subtle security flaws poses significant risks of financial loss and erosion of trust. LLMs create new opportunities f...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, CoT, arxiv, LLM, zero-shot | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Think First, Diffuse Fast: Improving Diffusion Language Model Reasoning via Autoregressive Plan Conditioning
item.description: arXiv:2603.13243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) generate text via iterative denoising but consistently underperform on multi-step reasoning. We hypothesize this gap stems from a coordination problem: AR models build coherence token-by-token, while diffusion m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, context, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ManiBench: A Benchmark for Testing Visual-Logic Drift and Syntactic Hallucinations in Manim Code Generation
item.description: arXiv:2603.13251v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traditional benchmarks like HumanEval and MBPP test logic and syntax effectively, but fail when code must produce dynamic, pedagogical visuals. We introduce ManiBench, a specialized benchmark evaluating LLM performance in generating Manim CE code, whe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, alignment, arxiv, LLM, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Translational Gaps in Graph Transformers for Longitudinal EHR Prediction: A Critical Appraisal of GT-BEHRT
item.description: arXiv:2603.13231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformer-based models have improved predictive modeling on longitudinal electronic health records through large-scale self-supervised pretraining. However, most EHR transformer architectures treat each clinical encounter as an unordered collection ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, transformer, paper, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Continual Fine-Tuning with Provably Accurate and Parameter-Free Task Retrieval
item.description: arXiv:2603.13235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Continual fine-tuning aims to adapt a pre-trained backbone to new tasks sequentially while preserving performance on earlier tasks whose data are no longer available. Existing approaches fall into two categories which include input- and parameter-adap...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, embedding, prompt, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Federated Personal Knowledge Graph Completion with Lightweight Large Language Models for Personalized Recommendations
item.description: arXiv:2603.13264v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Personalized recommendation increasingly relies on private user data, motivating approaches that can adapt to individuals without centralizing their information. We present Federated Targeted Recommendations with Evolving Knowledge graphs and Language...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, LLM, context, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Knowledge, Rules and Their Embeddings: Two Paths towards Neuro-Symbolic JEPA
item.description: arXiv:2603.13265v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern self-supervised predictive architectures excel at capturing complex statistical correlations from high-dimensional data but lack mechanisms to internalize verifiable human logic, leaving them susceptible to spurious correlations and shortcut le...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, RAG, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CAMEL-CLIP: Channel-aware Multimodal Electroencephalography-text Alignment for Generalizable Brain Foundation Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.13272v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) foundation models have shown promise for learning generalizable representations, yet they remain sensitive to channel heterogeneity, such as changes in channel composition or ordering. We propose channel-aware multimodal E...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, alignment, arxiv, experiment, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, context window, prompt engineering, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, RAG, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> knowledge base, model, LLM, framework, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, platform, framework, prompt, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Multi-Axis Trust Modeling for Interpretable Account Hijacking Detection
item.description: arXiv:2603.13246v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper proposes a Hadith-inspired multi-axis trust modeling framework, motivated by a structurally analogous problem in classical Hadith scholarship: assessing the trustworthiness of information sources using interpretable, multidimensional criter...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, context, framework, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Your Code Agent Can Grow Alongside You with Structured Memory
item.description: arXiv:2603.13258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While "Intent-oriented programming" (or "Vibe Coding") redefines software engineering, existing code agents remain tethered to static code snapshots. Consequently, they struggle to model the critical information embedded in the temporal evolution of p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, memory, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Attention: True Adaptive World Models via Spherical Kernel Operator
item.description: arXiv:2603.13263v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The pursuit of world model based artificial intelligence has predominantly relied on projecting high-dimensional observations into parameterized latent spaces, wherein transition dynamics are subsequently learned. However, this conventional paradigm i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, product, framework, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Spatially Aware Deep Learning for Microclimate Prediction from High-Resolution Geospatial Imagery
item.description: arXiv:2603.13273v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Microclimate models are essential for linking climate to ecological processes, yet most physically based frameworks estimate temperature independently for each spatial unit and rely on simplified representations of lateral heat exchange. As a result, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, context, framework, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: Automating Document Intelligence in Statutory City Planning
item.description: arXiv:2603.13245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: UK planning authorities face a legislative conflict between the Planning Act, which mandates public access to application documents, and the Data Protection Act, which requires protection of personal information. This situation creates a manually inte...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, framework, study, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ILION: Deterministic Pre-Execution Safety Gates for Agentic AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.13247v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The proliferation of autonomous AI agents capable of executing real-world actions - filesystem operations, API calls, database modifications, financial transactions - introduces a class of safety risk not addressed by existing content-moderation infra...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, vector, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Distilling Deep Reinforcement Learning into Interpretable Fuzzy Rules: An Explainable AI Framework
item.description: arXiv:2603.13257v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) agents achieve remarkable performance in continuous control but remain opaque, hindering deployment in safety-critical domains. Existing explainability methods either provide only local insights (SHAP, LIME) or employ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, step-by-step, prompt engineering, tool, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: A Dual-Path Generative Framework for Zero-Day Fraud Detection in Banking Systems
